---
title: "The Team"
subtitle: "Who built what"
toc: true
---

We are three MITB students at Singapore Management University, working on this
project for ISSS608 Visual Analytics and Applications. Each of us first
investigated the TenantThread embargo breach on our own in Take-home Exercise 2
and reached the same reading of the case, then combined our three approaches into
one investigation console, GLASSBOX.

We wrote the proposal, the minutes of meeting, and the user guide together, and
reviewed each other's work throughout. The build itself was divided into clear
sub-modules, each owned by one of us, so that the application is a set of well
defined parts rather than one person's work. The split is described below.

## Chen ZeXin

[Application architecture, System Health model, and website]{.gb-member-role}

Chen owned the overall shape of the application and the model at its centre.

- Designed the structure of the GLASSBOX application, the six destinations from
  Case through Verdict, and built it in R and Shiny with a separate module file
  for each tab.
- Built the System Health model that runs through the whole app, the four-meter
  composition and the score that tracks all 23 rounds, normalised to each force's
  worst moment.
- Built the Sandbox, the counterfactual engine that recomputes the breach-hour
  health as interventions are switched on and off.
- Designed the interaction model, the round slider, the click to jump health
  line, the five-step guided tour, and the interactive charts built with ggiraph.
- Built and styled the project website in Quarto, the forensic design system and
  the scroll animations, and handled deployment to shinyapps.io and Vercel.

## Meng Tingyu

[Data preparation and behavioural analysis]{.gb-member-role}

Tingyu owned the data foundation and the analysis of how the agents behaved.

- Led the data mining and preparation, reading the nested MC1 JSON without
  automatic simplification and flattening it into the tidy message, round, and
  reply tables that feed every part of the app.
- Diagnosed the contaminated stock field on the crisis day and established market
  sentiment as the clean backbone signal for the Pressure meter.
- Built the Transparency and Decision-making meters, the measure of how much
  traffic moved into hidden channels and the measure of out-of-role steering.
- Analysed the agents' private reasoning, tracing the drift from deliberation to
  reaction to justification and the language used to make the leak look compliant.
- Owned the Pressure, Transparency, and Decision-making content on the Vital Signs
  tab.

## Meng Xinyu

[Network, accountability, and the verdict]{.gb-member-role}

Xinyu owned the relational view of the team and the case for the conclusion.

- Built the reply network from the responding to links and computed the centrality
  measures, degree, betweenness, eigenvector, and closeness, with the calm weeks
  set against the crisis day.
- Built the Accountability meter, scoring the Judge's three duties of escalation,
  responsiveness, and restraint, and the heatmap of where the oversight failed.
- Developed the Verdict, the three competing explanations and the domino chain
  that the evidence supports, with the unlock logic that walks a user through it.
- Owned the Network and Verdict tabs.
- Led the design and layout of the project poster.
